title: The Tree of Knowledge
wikitext:
[]: The Tree of Knowledge             Quote Post by InterGrinch » Wed Dec 02, 2009 3:39 am What does the Tree of Knowledge mean to you? I once heard that the apple that Adam and Eve eat is allegorical for eating from the fruit of knowledge, which in turn led them out of the Garden of Eden, which represented ignorant bliss. I suppose that I subscribe to this theory, or at least it makes a lot of sense to me. I also think it's interesting to note that as a people, we have certainly not grown out the entire tree. But if we haven't finished our tree of knowledge, what does the end of the tree look like? Like instrumentality? Like that of Godhood?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Dec 02, 2009 1:58 pm Er....you do realize that the biblical symbolism and metaphors in Evangelion aren't real, right? Secondly, what the fuck are you babbling about? I honestly can't follow a word of this.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Dec 02, 2009 2:47 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Er....you do realize that the biblical symbolism and metaphors in Evangelion aren't real, right? Secondly, what the fuck are you babbling about? I honestly can't follow a word of this.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Wed Dec 02, 2009 3:08 pm LOLWUT? I think livejournal is for stringing together random thoughts.

[]: Re: The Tree of Knowledge             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Dec 02, 2009 6:32 pm InterGrinch wrote: What does the Tree of Knowledge mean to you?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Dec 02, 2009 7:21 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Er....you do realize that the biblical symbolism and metaphors in Evangelion aren't real, right?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Dec 02, 2009 8:27 pm A little courtesy goes a long way, especially towards new posters who come here asking questions. We were all looking for once. Most of us still are. In answer to InterGrinch's initial question, the Tree of Knowledge in Evangelion probably refers to knowledge itself and more specifically, the power of science. This is a power that humans possess, but Angel's do not. This is brought up in episode #12 during Fuyutsuki and Gendou's trip back from the South Pole Episode #12 wrote: FUYUTSUKI :The South Pole, a world of death which permits no life to exist. Or should I simply call it Hell? IKARI :Nevertheless, we, mankind, are here. We're living things, and we're alive. FUYUTSUKI :That's because we're under the protection of science. IKARI : Science is the power of man .

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Dec 02, 2009 8:40 pm That was a good post ^^^^^^ I just picture Sachiel with a smarty guy hat on and a science jacket. The fruit of Knowledge is held by Lilim which as you said and I believe to be Science. We can not live forever but we can build ways to last longer.

[]:             Quote Post by InterGrinch » Wed Dec 02, 2009 10:03 pm Thanks guys, that actually makes a lot of sense. I'm glad you weren't all mean to me

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Dec 03, 2009 3:56 am @OP: There's a good thread about this HERE in which I detail much of my analysis of the relevancy of the allusion. AuraTwilight wrote: Er....you do realize that the biblical symbolism and metaphors in Evangelion aren't real, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Dec 03, 2009 7:15 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: Er....you do realize that the biblical symbolism and metaphors in Evangelion aren't real, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Dec 03, 2009 8:06 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: That they're just for show.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Dec 03, 2009 3:14 pm W/hat I mean is that it's not literally true. The Angels aren't heavenly beings, the "Return to Eden" thing isn't literal, there's no Adam and Eve ending, etc. I still constantly see people like this.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Dec 03, 2009 3:46 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: BUT THEY'RE NOT!

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Thu Dec 03, 2009 4:09 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: But didn't someone who worked on the show say otherwise?

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Dec 03, 2009 4:09 pm ^ Stating that there is not an intended direct representation of Jdueo-Christian symbols is all well and good and prolly even accurate. His quote may even deflect possible accusations of heresy from some quarters (and offers a potential rational for making the statement upon NGE's world-wide availability). However, to say that the religious symbology is merely eye candy is perverse. Clearly there is meaning beyond "it looks cool", to deny there's any degree of meaning to the symbology used demands the plot elements of the show to become haphazardly, coincidentally, accidentally connected. Given enough time, a roomful of monkeys will deliver Shakespeare; NGE is no room of monkeys. It may be that the symbology used is not intended to be as deep as some of us seem to find it, but the way the symbols function from a literary, story-telling and philosophical Points of view make them more than mere unusual designs for the Japanese consumer. Anno may have chosen these symbols for their unfamiliarity to his initial audience; he did not then ignore their implications.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Dec 03, 2009 5:59 pm But what are those implications, if they even exist? While many seem to hold that the religious iconography in Evangelion has "meaning", there are very few examples of that meaning being shown clearly and even less of it being connected to the plot. Indeed, most analysis involving religious iconography ends up producing entirely misleading conclusions.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Dec 03, 2009 6:47 pm Merridian wrote: using something because it looks cool doesn't negate any potential for meaning.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Dec 03, 2009 6:53 pm No meaning that's intended to make a statement about Christian beliefs, yes, but they're definitely used in ways that take on meaning in the context of Evangelion itself, as a result of some combination of their original meaning that we already know and the meaning that is assigned to them in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Dec 03, 2009 7:19 pm AuraTwilight wrote: W/hat I mean is that it's not literally true. The Angels aren't heavenly beings, the "Return to Eden" thing isn't literal, there's no Adam and Eve ending, etc.
